source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Fraunces or a similar serif for honeycomb editorial display, Inter for product UI, IBM Plex Mono for data, and Noto Sans JP / Noto Serif JP guidance for Japanese typography.
layout: Precise 8px grid, 8px radii, honeycomb-cell panels, tan ridge borders, dark pore dividers, moss-green focus rings, and compact dashboard/data surfaces for catalog work.

prompts.label: Transparent Morel motif
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Morel transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only an isolated morel mushroom motif for a design-system catalog, no environment, no props, no text.
Subject: A single mature morel mushroom with a tall honeycomb cap, tan ochre ridges, dark hollow pits, warm brown stem, subtle moss-green reflected tones only in creases, accurate morel form, crisp silhouette edges, and natural spring woodland texture.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; show the full cap and stem without cropping; upright three-quarter view with clear honeycomb cells.
Lighting/mood: clean studio light that preserves honeycomb tan, dark hollow shadows, warm stem, and fine mushroom texture.
Chroma-key background: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for transparency removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no text, no watermark, no props, no forest floor, no leaves, no second mushroom, no insects, no landscape, no decorative background.

prompts.label: Light design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for "Morphous Morel" / "Morel Mushroom", derived from the isolated morel mushroom motif.
Reference use: Extract the palette, honeycomb cap texture, hollow pit shadows, tan ridges, warm stem fibers, and moss-green woodland accents from the morel motif. The board must visibly respect only motif-derived colors.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 4K-class layout, intended final asset 3840 x 2160.
Resolution and legibility: use large readable labels and spacious sections; avoid dense tiny text; no misspellings.
Required visible sections: title, motif story, palette, type, Japanese typography, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, tables, command/search, status, tokens, texture, radius, spacing, shadows.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; font guidance should fit multilingual product UI.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table, command menu, search bar, sidebar navigation, top navigation, dashboard charts, metric cards, empty state, error state, loading state.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, crisp realistic UI mockup, precise grid, 8px radii on cards and controls, tactile honeycomb cell panels, dark pit dividers, tan ridge borders, moss-green focus states, warm stem-paper surfaces.
Color palette: use only honeycomb tan, toasted ochre, dark hollow umber, stem cream, moss green, lichen olive, bark taupe, spore beige, and near-neutrals derived from the mushroom.
Text: short readable UI labels only; include section labels exactly where useful: Palette, Type, Japanese, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius 8px, Spacing, Shadows.
Constraints: no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple or blue gradients, no random brand names, no watermark, no decorative forest scene, no insects, no flowers, no extra mushrooms dominating the layout.

prompts.label: Dark design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for "Morphous Morel" / "Morel Mushroom", translated from the light board and the isolated morel motif. It must feel like the same system in dark mode, not a different theme.
Reference use: Preserve the morel honeycomb cap texture, hollow pit shadows, tan ridges, warm stem fibers, moss-green focus states, 8px radii, tactile cell panels, and shadcn/tweakcn product UI structure from the light board.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 4K-class layout, intended final asset 3840 x 2160.
Resolution and legibility: use large readable labels and spacious sections; avoid dense tiny text; no misspellings.
Required visible sections: title, Dark Mode, palette, type, Japanese typography, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, tables, command/search, status, tokens, texture, radius, spacing, shadows.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; font guidance should fit multilingual product UI.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table, command menu, search bar, sidebar navigation, top navigation, dashboard charts, metric cards, empty state, error state, loading state.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, crisp realistic UI mockup, precise grid, 8px radii on cards and controls, dark hollow umber background, spore beige foreground, toasted ochre primary actions, moss-green focus and success states, tan ridge borders, subtle pore-texture overlays.
Color palette: use only dark hollow umber, pit shadow, honeycomb tan, toasted ochre, stem cream, moss green, lichen olive, bark taupe, spore beige, and near-neutrals derived from the mushroom.
Text: short readable UI labels only; include section labels exactly where useful: Palette, Type, Japanese, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius 8px, Spacing, Shadows.
Constraints: no neon colors, no pure flat black, no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple or blue gradients, no random brand names, no watermark, no decorative forest scene, no insects, no flowers, no extra mushrooms dominating the layout.
